authoritySource: Dr. Ahmed Hesham — OET Listening Authoring Rulebook v1

rules.title: Exactly 42 items, contiguous numbering
rules.body: Every authored Listening paper has exactly 42 questions numbered 1..42 with no gaps. The shape is fixed: Part A1 (Q1-12), A2 (Q13-24), B (Q25-30), C1 (Q31-36), C2 (Q37-42).

rules.title: 24 / 6 / 12 split
rules.body: Part A must contain exactly 24 short-answer items (12 in A1 + 12 in A2). Part B must contain exactly 6 three-option MCQ items. Part C must contain exactly 12 three-option MCQ items (6 in C1 + 6 in C2). Any other shape is rejected by the publish gate.

rules.title: 30/42 ≡ 350/500 anchor
rules.body: Listening grading uses the canonical raw→scaled anchor 30/42 ≡ 350/500. Authors never produce or assert a scaled score; the OetScoring service computes it after submit.

rules.title: Part A items are short-answer, never MCQ
rules.body: Part A questions test note-taking from a fast consultation. They are short-answer / fill-in-the-blank only. MCQ is forbidden in Part A.

rules.title: Accept clinically-sensible spelling variants
rules.body: Each Part A item should provide the canonical answer plus 0-N accepted variants (UK/US spelling, common abbreviations, hyphen/space differences). Do NOT accept clinically-different terms.

rules.title: Stems carry a single, unambiguous gap
rules.body: Part A stems are partial note-form sentences with one blank to be completed. They never ask the candidate to write a full sentence.

rules.title: Part B is exactly 3-option MCQ
rules.body: Each Part B item has exactly 3 options labelled A, B, C. Two-option, four-option, multi-select, or true/false formats are forbidden.

rules.title: Part B targets gist / purpose / concern
rules.body: Part B questions probe the gist, speaker's purpose, or main concern of a workplace extract. Detail-trivia questions belong in Part A.

rules.title: Part C is exactly 3-option MCQ
rules.body: Each Part C item has exactly 3 options labelled A, B, C. Other shapes are forbidden.

rules.title: Part C tests inference, attitude, and detail across a longer extract
rules.body: Part C questions probe the speaker's attitude, implied meaning, or longer-range detail across a 6-minute presentation. Stems must be answerable only from the audio, never from general medical knowledge.

rules.title: Distractor categories are explicit
rules.body: Every wrong option in Part B / Part C should be tagged with one of: too_strong, too_weak, wrong_speaker, opposite_meaning, reused_keyword. Untagged distractors weaken downstream learner analytics.

rules.title: No giveaway distractors
rules.body: Wrong options must be plausible to a candidate who half-heard the audio. Avoid distractors that are obviously off-topic, ungrammatical, or contradict basic clinical knowledge.

rules.title: Reused keywords are a deliberate trap, not a coincidence
rules.body: When a wrong option re-uses a content word from the audio, mark its category as reused_keyword and explain why the reused word does NOT support the answer in the per-option why-wrong field.

rules.title: Every authored item carries a transcript excerpt
rules.body: Each question must include a verbatim excerpt from the audio script that supports the correct answer. Excerpts are not exposed to the learner pre-submit.

rules.title: Time-coded evidence enables jump-to-evidence playback
rules.body: When transcriptEvidenceStartMs / transcriptEvidenceEndMs are populated they must be non-negative, end > start, and reference the section audio (not the whole paper).

rules.title: Speaker attitude only on Part C
rules.body: speakerAttitude is only meaningful for Part C presentation extracts. Leave it null on Part A and Part B items.

rules.title: Speaker attitude vocabulary is fixed
rules.body: Allowed values: concerned | optimistic | doubtful | critical | neutral | other. Other tags must be rejected and surfaced as an authoring warning.

rules.title: Healthcare-realistic accents and registers
rules.body: Audio extracts represent realistic UK / IE / AU / NZ / CA healthcare environments. Accent must be intelligible at exam pace; registers are clinical (consultation, handover, presentation).

rules.title: One-play policy is not negotiable
rules.body: Listening papers are graded one-play only at exam time. Authoring drafts that depend on rewinding the same chunk twice violate the test format.
